kept.text: Louisville Gas and Electric Company and Kentucky Utilities Company (@lgeku), subsidiaries of PPL Corporation, and X-energy, Inc. are collaborating to explore deploying X-energy’s Xe-100 small modular reactor (SMR) in Kentucky to meet growing energy demand across the Commonwealth with long-term, reliable, clean energy. Read more: https://t.co/aSKatubDR7

profile_bio.description: Advanced Nuclear Energy to Empower Earth

kept.text:
KENTUCKY MOVES TOWARD SMRs WITH X-energy

· LG&E and KU (PPL) partnering with X-energy to explore Xe-100 deployment
· Focus: grid reliability + rising data center demand
· Backing: Kentucky Site Readiness Program offering $75M total, up to $25M per project

THE TECH
· Xe-100 = 80 MWe high-temperature gas reactor
· 4-pack = 320 MWe, scalable to 12-unit (gigawatt-scale)
· Modular build, each unit brought online independently

Kentucky isn't talking theory anymore:
· Nuclear Development Authority (2024)
· PSC investigation (2025)
· Site Readiness law (April 2026)

State policy → project pipeline → utility engagement

That's how SMRs actually get built.

$XE $PPL $URNM
